chapter_title.ar: باب الزَّكَاةِ تُبْعَثُ مِنْ بَلَدٍ إِلَى بَلَدٍ أَوْ تُدْفَعُ إِلَى مَنْ يَقْسِمُهَا فَتَضِيعُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 35 – The Zakāt being sent from a city to a city, or is handed over to someone to distribute it, so it gets wasted

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/101)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/101)
text: إِذَا أَخْرَجَ الرَّجُلُ الزَّكَاةَ مِنْ مَالِهِ ثُمَّ سَمَّاهَا لِقَوْمٍ فَضَاعَتْ أَوْ أَرْسَلَ بِهَا إِلَيْهِمْ فَضَاعَتْ فَلَا شَيْ‏ءَ عَلَيْهِ .

en.hubeali: Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz, from Abu Baseer,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’farasws having said: ‘When the man extracts the Zakāt from his wealth, then specifies it for a particular group of people, but it gets wasted, or he sends with it/him to them, but it gets wasted, so there is nothing upon him’.
en.sarwar: 2. Hammad ibn ‘Isa has narrated from Hariz from abu Basir who has said the following: “Abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one takes out his Zakat and assigns it to be paid to a people but it is lost, or he sends it to them and it is lost, he is not held responsible.’”
